interface.main_functions: Every Arduino sketch needs setup() for initialization and loop() for the main logic that runs forever.
interface.tinkercad_specifics: In Tinkercad, you can find the 'Code' button on the top right. Use the 'Blocks + Text' view to see how the blocks translate to C++ code.

technical_constraints.digital_pins: Pins 0 to 13. Pin 13 usually has a built-in LED.
technical_constraints.analog_pins: A0 to A5. Used for reading sensors like potentiometers or light sensors (LDR).

tips: Always add a 'delay(100)' in loops to prevent the simulation from crashing or running too fast.
Connect the long leg of the LED (anode) to the signal pin and the short leg (cathode) to GND with a 220-ohm resistor.
